name: Vtiger CRM API
what_it_does: Vtiger CRM REST API — open-source and cloud CRM with sales, support, and marketing modules in one platform, enabling agents to manage contacts, deals, cases, and campaigns across the unified Vtiger CRM suite.
use_cases: Agents managing the full sales pipeline in Vtiger — creating leads, updating deals, and logging sales activities
Customer support integration — agents creating Vtiger cases from external ticketing systems and monitoring SLA compliance
Contact data management — agents syncing customer records between Vtiger and external data sources or enrichment services
Marketing automation — agents triggering Vtiger marketing campaigns based on CRM segment criteria
Workflow automation — agents reading Vtiger data to generate reports and trigger downstream business processes
not_for: Modern developer-first teams — Vtiger's API is older-style; use HubSpot or Attio for better developer experience
Enterprise-scale CRM — Salesforce is more appropriate for large enterprise with complex customization requirements
Teams without CRM history with Vtiger — HubSpot, Pipedrive, or Close offer better onboarding for new CRM deployments
best_when: You already use Vtiger CRM and need to automate workflows across its combined sales, support, and marketing modules with a single API.
avoid_when: You're selecting a new CRM — modern alternatives like HubSpot, Pipedrive, or Attio have far better developer APIs and ecosystems.

auth.notes: Basic HTTP authentication with username and access key (from Vtiger settings). Token-based auth also available for Vtiger Cloud. No scope granularity — auth tied to user role permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source edition: free (self-hosted); Vtiger Cloud: free trial
pricing.notes: Open source edition (Vtiger 7) is free to self-host with full API access. Cloud edition has paid tiers with hosted infrastructure. Open source allows unlimited customization.

performance.rate_limits: No published rate limits; self-hosted: server capacity dependent; cloud: practical limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is dated and incomplete in places. Error responses are functional but terse. Module-based API design requires understanding Vtiger's module system. Self-hosted version docs differ from Cloud docs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Record creation is not idempotent — duplicates can be created without deduplication. Updates by record ID are idempotent. Search-before-create pattern required for deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Vtiger uses module-based API — agents must use correct module names (Contacts, Potentials, HelpDesk) in API calls
Record IDs use module-prefixed format (12x456) — agents must handle this format when referencing related records
Self-hosted vs Cloud API differ — some endpoints and behaviors vary between deployment types
Relationship management requires separate retrieve_related API calls — not embedded in record responses
Custom modules and fields require querying available_modules endpoint first — schema is dynamic and installation-specific

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant on cloud edition. TLS on cloud; self-hosted security depends on deployment. Basic auth with no scope granularity. Open source — security depends on deployment practices. GDPR data processing options available.
